postData.contentHtml:
Houston has been buzzing with World Cup fever, as soccer fans flock to NRG Stadium (dubbed Houston Stadium for the tournament) in droves. With several matches already declared official sellouts and attendance figures consistently soaring over 68,000, the energy is undeniable. However, a surprising fact has emerged: despite the immense popularity, none of the World Cup games in Houston have managed to break into the city's top 10 biggest soccer crowds in history.

The reason lies in FIFA's stringent regulations. For World Cup matches, stadium capacities are capped at 68,777, and crucially, standing-room-only sections—which have historically allowed Houston to pack in even more spectators for other events—are not permitted. This strict cap means that while World Cup games are undoubtedly drawing massive support, they can't quite reach the dizzying heights of past attendance records.

Houston's rich soccer history boasts several events that have seen even larger turnouts. The city has hosted 11 soccer matches with over 70,000 fans, many of them featuring the beloved Mexico national team. For instance, the 2025 Gold Cup final holds the all-time record with an astounding 70,925 attendees. Other events like the 2016 Copa América semifinal (70,858) and various Gold Cup quarterfinals have also surpassed the current World Cup capacity limits.

Even with the capacity restrictions, the social sentiment around the World Cup in Houston remains overwhelmingly positive, reflecting the city's vibrant soccer culture and the excitement of hosting such a prestigious tournament. While there's some debate regarding these capacity limitations, the enthusiasm from fans is clear, cementing Houston's status as a major soccer hub. The World Cup might not be setting new attendance records, but it's undoubtedly adding another thrilling chapter to Houston's storied soccer legacy.
postData.title: Houston's World Cup Paradox: Massive Crowds, But Not Record-Breaking
postData.summary: Despite drawing colossal crowds, none of Houston's World Cup matches have cracked the city's top 10 historical soccer attendance records. Find out why FIFA regulations are keeping these games just shy of the top spots.

sources.title: 20 biggest soccer crowds in Houston history and why World Cup doesn't have any in Top 10
